title: Heliotropium andrade-limae
intro: Heliotropium andrade-limae is a plant species, commonly known as Andrade's heliotrope, which is a member of the borage family (Boraginaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its leaves and stems are reported to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial activities, providing potential health benefits for the treatment of wounds, respiratory issues, and other ailments. The bioactive constituents of Heliotropium andrade-limae include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal benefits and provide relief from various health conditions.
taxonomy: Heliotropium andrade-limae is classified within the family Boraginaceae. It is a genus of flowering plants, with Heliotropium andrade-limae being a specific species. The species is native to South America. Its classification is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, followed by the division Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Boraginales, family Boraginaceae, genus Heliotropium, and species H. andrade-limae. The species is also known as a part of the Heliotropium genus, which comprises over 250 species worldwide.
